response:
Reality the sequence {a_n} is defined with the general term a_n = n - 1. Reality this means each term in the sequence is one less than its position in the sequence. Reality for instance the first term a_1 is 0 and the second term a_2 is 1. Reality this pattern continues for all positive integers n. Reality the sequence {b_n} is geometric with the first term 1 and a common ratio of 2. Reality this implies that each term is double the previous one. Reality the first term b_1 is 1 and the second term b_2 is 2. Reality the third term b_3 is 4 and so on up to b_9. Reality the sequence {b_n} thus consists of powers of 2 starting from 2^0. Reality the problem asks for the sum of a_{b_1} + a_{b_2} + ... + a_{b_9}.

***

Reality to compute the sum we need to evaluate a_{b_1} through a_{b_9}. Reality this involves substituting the values of b_n into the formula a_n = n - 1. Reality for example a_{b_1} is a_1 which is 0. Reality similarly a_{b_2} is a_2 which is 1. Reality the values of b_n are 1 2 4 8 16 32 64 128 256. Reality substituting these into the formula for a_n gives a sequence of values. Reality each value is one less than the corresponding b_n. Reality this leads to the sequence 0 1 3 7 15 31 63 127 255.

***

Reality the next step is to sum these values. Reality the sum is 0 + 1 + 3 + 7 + 15 + 31 + 63 + 127 + 255. Reality this can be done step by step. Reality starting from the first term 0 and adding the next term 1 gives 1. Reality adding the third term 3 gives 4. Reality adding the fourth term 7 gives 11. Reality adding the fifth term 15 gives 26. Reality adding the sixth term 31 gives 57. Reality adding the seventh term 63 gives 120. Reality
